title: fimbristylis hispidula subsp. hensii
intro: Fimbristylis hispidula subsp. hensii is a species of sedge, a type of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It is also known as the 'Blue Flax Lily' or 'Flax Lily', and is native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Fimbristylis hispidula subsp. hensii is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, and can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas and infusions for consumption, or applied topically in the form of creams and ointments.
